title: Silky Russian Chicken Stir fry
description: A plant-based-friendly, umami-rich Russian dish featuring chicken breasts, perfect for a hangover cure. This silky stir-fry combines the savory flavors of Russian cuisine with the comfort of a warm, nourishing meal.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Add the sliced onions to the skillet and cook until they are translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, tomato paste, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Pour the sauce over the onions and mushrooms in the skillet.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and stir to coat with the sauce.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, for 10-12 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the crumbled silken tofu and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the tofu is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
